speaker: doctor
text: I see you're currently taking Lisinopril 20mg once daily. How have you been feeling on this medication?

speaker: patient
text: Overall, I think it's been okay, but lately I've noticed some irregular heartbeats.

speaker: doctor
text: Irregular heartbeats can be concerning. Let's check your vital signs to understand better.

speaker: doctor
text: Your heart rate is elevated at 108 beats per minute. That might explain the palpitations.

speaker: patient
text: Yes, that's exactly what I've been feeling—my heart sometimes races unexpectedly.

speaker: doctor
text: I also noticed your body temperature is slightly high at 38.3 degrees Celsius. Are you experiencing any fever or chills?

speaker: patient
text: I have felt a bit feverish and sweaty for the past day or so.

speaker: doctor
text: Your diastolic blood pressure is 104 mmHg, which is on the higher side. This could be contributing to your symptoms.

speaker: patient
text: That might be why I've been feeling palpitations and a bit dizzy at times.

speaker: doctor
text: Given your symptoms and test results, I believe your primary diagnosis is Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus. We should address that as well.

speaker: doctor
text: By the way, your temperature now reads 37.4 degrees Celsius, which is a bit lower than before.

speaker: doctor
text: I'm going to switch your medication from Lisinopril to Amlodipine 10mg daily to better control your blood pressure.

speaker: patient
text: Is this new medication going to help with my heart rate and blood pressure issues?

speaker: doctor
text: Amlodipine should help lower your blood pressure, which in turn may reduce your heart palpitations and improve your heart rate.

speaker: doctor
text: Your heart rate now has decreased to 87 beats per minute, which is an improvement compared to earlier.

speaker: doctor
text: Your diastolic blood pressure has also normalized to 110 mmHg, showing a better trend.

speaker: patient
text: That's good to hear. Should I monitor my blood pressure and heart rate at home?

speaker: doctor
text: Yes, please keep a daily record of your blood pressure and heart rate. Also, monitor your blood sugar levels closely.

speaker: patient
text: I will do that. Are there any other symptoms I should watch out for?

speaker: patient
text: Actually, my temperature is back to 38.3 degrees Celsius again this evening, which worries me a bit.
